policy.text: Handle invalid inputs explicitly and return the required sentinel value instead of allowing conversion errors to escape.
Infer output ordering from the tests as part of the contract, and verify examples exactly instead of assuming “unique” means preserving input order.

curve.lesson: Always identify and explicitly handle boundary cases—especially empty inputs—before applying operations that assume nonempty data.
Handle invalid inputs explicitly and return the required sentinel value instead of allowing conversion errors to escape.
Infer output ordering from the tests as part of the contract, and verify examples exactly instead of assuming “unique” means preserving input order.
Match the output’s required precision and formatting—when examples expect `33.33`, round percentage results to two decimal places.
